cards.front: What is the main thread in Java?
cards.back: The main thread is the initial thread started when a Java application begins execution.

cards.front: What is the difference between a new thread and a new Runnable?
cards.back: A new thread is an instance of the Thread class, while a new Runnable is an instance of the Runnable interface.

cards.front: How does a thread join another thread?
cards.back: A thread joins another thread using the join() method of the target thread.

cards.front: What is the difference between a thread and a process?
cards.back: A thread is a lightweight process that shares the same memory space as the parent process, while a process is an independent entity with its own memory space.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the Thread constructor in Java?
cards.back: The Thread constructor is used to specify the thread name, group, and priority when creating a new thread.
